name: Alice Chess
author: V. R. Parton (1953) / AGP port
description: Alice Chess (V. R. Parton, 1953) — "through the looking-glass" chess on TWO boards. All pieces start on board A; board B starts empty. A piece moves by its normal chess move on the board it occupies, then transfers to the corresponding square on the OTHER board — and the move is legal only if that mirror square is vacant. Captures happen on the moving board; check is judged on the board the king stands on. Checkmate wins.
